name: Google Gemma
what_it_does: Google's family of open-weight lightweight LLMs (1B-27B) for on-device, edge, and self-hosted inference without sending data to Google.
use_cases: Run privacy-preserving AI inference on-device without sending data to external APIs
Deploy custom fine-tuned models in air-gapped or regulated environments
Build cost-effective agent pipelines where per-token cloud API costs are prohibitive at scale
Fine-tune for domain-specific tasks using Gemma's open weights with LoRA or full fine-tuning
Prototype agent systems using Gemma on Kaggle/Colab for free GPU access before production
not_for: Production inference without infrastructure investment — requires GPU/TPU for reasonable throughput
Tasks requiring frontier model capabilities (complex reasoning, long context) where Gemini/Claude outperform
Teams without ML engineering expertise to manage model serving infrastructure
best_when: Best for privacy-sensitive workloads, edge deployment, or high-volume inference where self-hosting beats per-token API costs.
avoid_when: Avoid when frontier-quality output is needed and infrastructure cost/complexity of self-hosting is not justified.

auth.notes: Open weights — download from Kaggle/HuggingFace Hub. Kaggle requires account for downloads. HuggingFace requires accepting license.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free to download and self-host; Kaggle provides free GPU notebooks
pricing.paid_tiers: Infrastructure costs for self-hosted deployment; Gemma also available via Google AI Studio
pricing.notes: Model weights are free under Gemma Terms of Use (not OSI open source). Commercial use permitted with restrictions.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Inference errors depend on serving framework (Ollama, HuggingFace, vLLM) — not Gemma-specific.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Inference is stateless — same inputs produce equivalent (not identical) outputs with temperature > 0.
known_agent_gotchas: Gemma license terms prohibit certain uses (weapons, illegal activities) — check terms before deployment even though weights are 'open'
Gemma 3 multimodal models require vision-capable serving infrastructure — text-only serving code will fail with image inputs
Different quantization formats (Q4_K_M, Q8_0, BF16) have significant quality/speed tradeoffs — benchmark for your use case
Gemma instruction-tuned models expect specific chat template format — raw completion models respond differently without proper prompt formatting
Gemma 1B/2B models have limited context retention and reasoning — don't expect GPT-4 quality on complex multi-step agent tasks

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted means full data control. Verify license terms for commercial production use.
